frozen_components: base Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model weights (adapted via LoRA)
architecture.summary: VersaPRM is a Process Reward Model (PRM) that scores the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps in Chain-of-Thought (CoT) sequences. It takes a sequence of reasoning steps as input and outputs a vector of reward scores, one per step. The model is initialized from a math-domain PRM (LlamaPRM800K based on Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct) and fine-tuned via LoRA on synthetic multi-domain reasoning data. At inference, aggregated step scores guide reranking methods (Majority Voting, Weighted Majority Voting, Best-of-N) and search algorithms (Beam Search, MCTS) to select high-quality solutions from multiple candidate CoTs.
trained_components: LoRA adapters for multi-domain PRM fine-tuning

biblio.title: VersaPRM: Multi-Domain Process Reward Model via Synthetic Reasoning Data

claims.statement: current PRMs have poor performance in other domains. Math PRMs trained exclusively on mathematical data lack the versatility to generalize beyond mathematical reasoning tasks.
in the MMLU-Pro category of Law, VersaPRM via weighted majority voting achieves a 7.9% performance gain over the majority voting baseline
Qwen2.5-Math-PRM's gain in the MMLU-Pro category of Law is 1.3%
For CoTs labeled as correct by the auto-labeler, agreement rate with manual evaluations was 80% (95% CI: 0.69–0.89); for CoTs labeled as incorrect, agreement rate was 71% (95% CI: 0.63–0.79)
VersaPRM consistently outperforms open-source math PRMs in both WMV and BoN across all domain groups (Math, Math-adjacent, and non-Math-adjacent domains)

VersaPRM trained with one domain held out (Law or Biology) remains comparable to the fully trained model across both evaluated domains and surpasses the performance of math PRMs, demonstrating genuine domain-general reasoning capabilities
VersaPRM (random subset) achieves higher WMV accuracy across both math and non-math tasks compared to VersaPRM (Math subset), demonstrating the value of training on diverse data
The CoT generation and labeling pipeline for MMLU-Pro-CoT-Train (Labeled) was completed at a total cost under $100 USD using AWS Bedrock batch inference
